title: Create dark-mode project task tracker UI
summary: Create a small Astro-generated, completely read-only tracker under workflow-foundry that helps review all repo projects and their JSON task state, includes light/dark mode and current-project-only toggles, and does not replace the canonical project/task JSON source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: grilling identifies the intended operator workflow and the project/task fields the tracker must surface
the PRD defines the HTML tracker data source, light/dark mode toggle behavior, and completely read-only boundaries
the UI shows all projects by default and includes a toggle or filter to view only the current project
grilling resolves whether connected-to-JSON means a generated snapshot or a runtime app/server that reads JSON
the implementation surface is Astro static generation scoped to the workflow-foundry project
issues split the UI, data loading, validation, and review work into independently implementable slices
the implemented tracker renders projects, tasks, status, task kind, Matt phase, next action, and linked artifacts from repo JSON state
the tracker is validated with a deterministic fixture or script and does not introduce Python

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, all non-done tasks, and Matt/ECC workflow references before shaping or implementing the dark-mode HTML tracker.

approved_artifacts.approval_note: Approved by user instruction to implement the Astro tracker inside workflow-foundry.

session_log.event: Created intake task for a dark-mode HTML project/task tracker UI.
Moved to grilling and recorded user requirement: tracker should be a small HTML file with a toggle between dark mode and light mode.
Recorded user requirement: tracker must be completely read-only and must not edit project or task JSON from the browser.
Recorded user requirement: tracker should show all projects by default and include a toggle/filter to view only the current project.
Recorded design fork: user suspects a different framework may be needed if the tracker should stay connected to JSON at runtime.

Recorded framework discussion: Astro is the recommended lightweight static-generation direction if the tracker remains a generated read-only snapshot; lightweight React/Vite remains an option if runtime app behavior becomes more important.
Resolved framework and placement: use Astro for the generated read-only tracker and keep it inside the workflow-foundry project.
Wrote PRD for the Astro-generated read-only project/task tracker and moved task to PRD phase with issues as the next action.
Published four local issue artifacts for Astro shell, all-project rendering, view controls, and validation/build workflow; moved task to issues phase with implement as the next action.
Moved to implement and approved Astro tracker package, config, data loader, page, test, lockfile, and local ignore artifacts after user said to build it.
Approved generated-output test artifact to verify representative content in the built Astro tracker snapshot.

Implemented the Astro read-only tracker, data loader, package scripts, data tests, generated-output test, and local ignore rules; verification, audit, workflow validation, and dev-server response checks passed.
Updated snapshot to include workflow-foundry-006 after adding the foundry expansion discovery intake task.
Closed during cleanup after the read-only Astro tracker was superseded by the tracker UI runner/project-view task and tracker verification was stabilized against current task counts.
